name: Fillets (or Steaks) of Walleye Mornay
recipeIngredient: 1/2 tsp Tarragon OR Pinch of cayenne
recipeInstructions:
1. Place fillets or steaks in a saucepan, just cover with cold water. Add salt, lemon juice, and cayenne. Bring to a simmer.
2. Remove fish, retaining the fish broth. Place fish in a buttered baking dish.
3. Stir together 1 cup fish broth and cream.
4. Melt 3 tablespoons of butter and add minced onion, tarragon or marjoram. Cook until onions are soft.
5. Stir in flour and cook for a minute or two. Turn off the heat.
6. Gradually stir in the remaining fish broth until smooth. Return to low heat, stirring until sauce thickens.
7. Spoon 2-3 tablespoons of the sauce into the beaten eggs. Stir the eggs slowly into the sauce, adding cheese. Continue cooking until uniform.
8. Pour the sauce over the fish and dot with remaining butter. Bake in a preheated oven at 375°F for about 10 minutes to brown the sauce.
